name: Adam Harris
title: Co-Founder & COO
notes: Co-Founder & COO of AnswerAI and Last Rev. 20+ years of engineering, sales, and business experience. Contentful Certified Professional. Interviewed at Netlify's Compose Conference (Fairmont San Francisco) about sophisticated website strategies. Previously COO at 195 Places, Principal at THarris Consultants. Purdue University alum. GitHub account since Jan 2013 with 13 public repos spanning Rust, JavaScript, PHP, and Docker. Currently building AlphaClaw command center platform — actively directing AI agent development, CRM/team directory features, and shared component architecture. 50,905 Slack messages in workspace.

insights.summary:
Adam Harris is a hands-on technical co-founder who operates at the intersection of engineering leadership, product management, and AI agent development. With 20+ years of experience across engineering, sales, and business, he's deeply embedded in the code — reviewing PRs with exceptional thoroughness (his PR review on the Sanity native architecture PR #798 was a masterclass in structured code review). He currently splits his focus between AnswerAI (AI agent platform) and Last Rev (enterprise CMS consultancy), while actively building AlphaClaw, a personal AI-powered command center.

His recent Slack activity (50,905 total messages) reveals a product-minded builder who thinks in systems and shared components. He directs AI agent development with rapid-fire instructions — building CRM features, team research workflows, shared UI components (pills, tabs, modals, filters), and debugging tools. He frequently tests on mobile, catches UI bugs immediately, and expects instant iteration. He's the kind of founder who files bugs while taking his kids to school.

Adam's communication style is ultra-concise and directive — short sentences, one idea per message, heavy use of 'should' and 'need'. He doesn't explain why; he states what he wants and trusts execution. He's direct but not harsh, says 'TY' and 'IMO', and escalates through clarity rather than emotion. He tags people by name for accountability and prefers Slack over all other channels. His GitHub shows interests spanning Rust (Rojo, land2port), Next.js, WordPress/PHP, Salesforce, and Stripe — reflecting a polyglot engineering background. He's Contentful Certified and spoke at Netlify's Compose Conference.

personal: Roblox/game dev tools (Rojo fork in Rust)
Video processing (land2port — landscape to portrait converter)

stressors: unclear requirements
slow PR cycles
broken mobile experiences
UI inconsistencies
tools not using shared components
having to repeat instructions

conversationStarters: The AlphaClaw command center he's building — what's the vision for the full platform?
His approach to AI-directed development — how does he find working with an AI agent full-time?
The Rojo fork — is he exploring Roblox game development?
land2port (Rust) — a landscape-to-portrait video converter with face tracking, interesting side project
His talk at Netlify Compose — what resonated most with the audience?
The CRM/team directory he's designing — building a mini-Salesforce with personality insights
insights.bestApproach: Keep messages extremely concise — one idea per message. Lead with the decision or action needed, not background context. He responds fastest on Slack during afternoon PT hours. For technical discussions, come prepared with specifics — he'll spot gaps immediately. Don't over-explain; he prefers to ask questions if he needs more context. Use shared components and established patterns — he notices when you don't. When reporting bugs, include the exact UI state and what's wrong.

name: Brad Taylor
title: Co-Founder & CEO

companies.name: Beats Me
companies.notes: Music attribution platform, backed by Marc Benioff

companies.name: AlphaClaw

notes: Co-Founder & CEO of AnswerAI (answeragent.ai) and Last Rev. Creator of AlphaClaw — AI-powered personal agent platform deployed across the team. Previously Web Development Manager at Optimizely — guided Fortune 500 companies (NYT, Microsoft, Apple, Priceline) on data-driven content/marketing. Purdue University alum. Founded Tech Stands Up (civil rights org, 2017-2019) — raised $15K, 1000+ attendees, featured in Forbes Under 30 Network. DJ and music producer for 25+ years. Founded Beats Me (backed by Marc Benioff) for artist attribution. Runs YouTube channel 'Digital at Scale' and digitalatscale.com (AI agent course platform). Speaker at PR Daily/Ragan events on AI for communicators. Helped launch early websites for NBC and the Smithsonian. GitHub bio: 'Founder and CEO of AnswerAgentAI building an open, creative, and decentralized future you can trust.' 16,188+ Slack messages in workspace. Actively shipping AlphaClaw dashboard updates, monitoring infrastructure, and managing client accounts (Kumello, SBI, IAS, WOW). Starred firecrawl and openclaw repos on GitHub (Feb 2026). Runs enterprise AI webinar series 'Bridging the GenAI Divide'. Recently merged MCP credential pattern fix for AnswerAgent and alphaagent-team account report slides.

insights.summary:
Brad Taylor (Bradley Taylor) is the CEO and Co-Founder of AnswerAI/AnswerAgent and Last Rev, based in San Francisco. He's a visionary product-CEO who operates at the intersection of AI, enterprise software, and go-to-market strategy. With 16,188+ Slack messages, he's the most prolific communicator in the workspace — deeply hands-on across engineering, product, client management, and infrastructure.

His recent activity (Feb 2026) shows him in full builder-CEO mode: he deployed AlphaClaw Dashboard v2 to all team instances with a comprehensive feature update (7-channel support including WhatsApp, Slack, Discord, Telegram, Signal, Google Chat, MS Teams), set up monitoring/alerting infrastructure, personally diagnosed and fixed team instances (disk full, config issues), expanded storage for team members, and managed client relationships (Kumello launch alignment, SBI sync, WOW contract crisis). On GitHub, he merged MCP credential pattern fixes for AnswerAgent, built account report slides for alphaagent-team, and starred firecrawl and openclaw repos.

In Zoom meetings, Brad is warm and personable — he opens with casual banter (Valentine's Day, Costco stories, travel), but quickly pivots to strategic business discussions. He's empathetic with contractors ('I'm open to structuring payments, risk, just time') and collaborative in client calls. He asks probing questions and listens carefully before responding. His meeting style is facilitative rather than dominating — he lets others speak, then synthesizes and directs.

His Slack messages reveal the classic Brad pattern: rapid-fire short messages, casual typos (teh, rto, ahve), immediate problem-solving, and frequent @mentions for accountability. He announces updates with @channel and rich formatting, shares links constantly, and creates video walkthroughs for complex topics. He's excited about what the team builds ('LFG! Im excited to see what the team build with it!') and protective of team resources ('maybe we need an alpha claw fails channel'). He also floats ambitious product ideas: 'We could setup a public telegram or whats app where people could text a question and get a full report back.'

Before founding his companies, Brad was at Optimizely guiding Fortune 500 companies on data-driven strategies. He helped launch early websites for NBC and the Smithsonian. He's a DJ/music producer of 25+ years, founded Beats Me (backed by Marc Benioff), founded Tech Stands Up (civil rights, Forbes feature), and runs Digital at Scale (AI agent education platform). He speaks at PR Daily/Ragan events and runs an enterprise AI webinar series 'Bridging the GenAI Divide.'
communicationStyle: casual-with-typos (teh, rto, ahve, canb — types fast, does not edit)
warm-opener-then-business (casual banter → strategic pivot in meetings)
self-deprecating-humor (jokes about team overusing his tools)
insights.bestApproach: Brad is a fast-moving CEO who context-switches constantly between AlphaClaw infrastructure, client management, and product strategy. Lead with the headline — what's the status, what's the blocker, what do you need. He responds to urgency and appreciates proactive updates. For technical issues, include specific error details — he reads logs himself and personally diagnoses instance health. For client-facing work, he values polished documentation with video walkthroughs. He's most active on Slack and expects quick responses. Don't make him chase you for updates — he'll call it out publicly. Frame ideas in terms of product/customer impact. He's in PT timezone but works irregular hours including late nights. In meetings, he's warm and personable — open with casual chat, then get strategic. He's generous with time and resources ('I updated everyone's storage space this morning').
motivators: shipping AlphaClaw and AnswerAgent platform features
closing client deals (Kumello, SBI, Aging Navigator)
creating polished client-facing deliverables (docs, videos, dashboards)
ethical AI, civic tech, and creator attribution
ambitious product ideas (public Telegram/WhatsApp AI bots, congress.gov integration)
stressors: disengaged team members (cameras off, no engagement)
client churn risk (code red — every vendor being questioned)
runaway AI costs
production outages affecting team instances
config errors from bots editing their own configs
slow responses and having to chase for updates
team members running out of resources (disk, memory)
security incidents

professional: AI agents and LLM infrastructure (AnswerAgent, Flowise, MCP)
enterprise AI webinars (Bridging the GenAI Divide)
personal: civic tech (Tech Stands Up, ai-sfgov, Voter Sidekick)
ethical AI and creator rights (Beats Me)
family life (Valentine's Day toy stories in Zoom calls)
sharedWithAdam: Last Rev operations and client management

stressors: disengagement
churn-risk
slow-responses

conversationStarters: The AlphaClaw Dashboard v2 rollout — 7-channel support is impressive, what channels are getting the most use?
His idea for a public Telegram/WhatsApp bot with congress.gov integration — is he pursuing that?
The Kumello launch alignment — how is the data engine coming along?
His Digital at Scale AI agent course — what modules are launching next?
The enterprise AI webinar Bridging the GenAI Divide — how was attendance?

Avoid discussing client contract risks in public channels
Don't ask about salary/raise discussions — handled 1:1

name: Cameron Taylor
title: Site Reliability Engineer
notes: SRE at Last Rev. Active on Diligent, IAS, and SmartNews projects. Works on npm packages (publishes under @cameronlr). Handles PR reviews/merges for client repos (e.g. diligent-marketing). QA-minded — frequently surfaces bugs before prod merges. Personal email: ct3685@gmail.com. GitHub blog links to lastrev.com. 8 public repos.

insights.summary: Cameron is a hands-on SRE who serves as the operational backbone of Last Rev's client projects. He manages deployments, troubleshoots environments, coordinates with external clients (Diligent, IAS, Impossible Foods, Lively), and handles support tickets across multiple accounts simultaneously. His communication style is direct, conversational, and unfiltered — he says what he thinks and isn't afraid to push back when he feels micromanaged or when instructions are unclear. He uses casual language, emojis (🤷‍♂️, 😎, 🤔), ellipses, and code blocks naturally. He's the person who keeps production running and clients unblocked.
insights.bestApproach: Be explicit and specific when requesting deployments or changes — he's been burned by vague instructions and will push back if details are missing ('I need EXPLICIT details on what I'm deploying'). Don't micromanage his workflow or ticket statuses — he values autonomy and gets frustrated when others adjust his work items. He responds well to direct, peer-level communication. For urgent items, tag him directly. He's in Eastern/Indiana timezone. When debugging, come with screenshots and specifics — he'll ask for them anyway.
motivators: autonomy over his workflow and tickets
building useful tools (watchtower, go-links, family-feud game)
being the go-to person who gets things done
stressors: vague deployment instructions without specific branches/commits
being micromanaged or having ticket statuses changed by others
unclear ownership of tasks between team members
network/internet issues (Indiana weather)
passive-aggressive communication from colleagues
repeated requests for things already communicated
conversationStarters: The family-feud game he built — fun personal project with lots of recent commits
skiwithcare.com — does he ski competitively or is it a community thing?
His watchtower healthcheck toolkit — interesting open-source SRE tool
The ethical-h4cker repo fork — is he pursuing security certifications?
Managing deployments across so many client accounts — how does he keep it all straight?
topicsToAvoid: Don't critique his ticket management workflow — he's protective of his process
Avoid vague or hand-wavy deployment requests — always specify exact branches and environments
Don't imply he missed something he already addressed — he tracks his work carefully

name: Maximiliano Techera
title: Director of Engineering
notes: Independent fullstack engineer, writer, professor, and coffee enthusiast based in Montevideo, Uruguay. BS in Computer Science with 10+ years experience. Toptal network member. Personal site: maxtechera.me (DESIGN | CODE | ARCHITECTURE — currently down). Manages engineering across Last Rev client projects (IAS, SmartNews, Diligent) and AnswerAI product. Handles releases, deployments, billing (Stripe), Langfuse tracing, npm publishing, and team coordination. Also teaches — has university teaching repos (DIU-Class-2021, N6A-NA-2019.2) and recently created app-workshop-notas-ia (AI notes workshop, Jan 2026). GitHub shows 53 public repos, 18 followers. Recently very active on the-answerai/theanswer — merging PRs for MCP credential patterns, marketplace fixes, metrics filters, version history auth, IAS security CVEs, and typecheck enablement (Feb 2026). Active on Claude Code plugins and AI tooling (claude-plugins, solo-claude, claude-max repos). Currently lists company as 'AnswerAI' on GitHub. Uses Zen Browser (commented on passkey freeze issue). Co-creator of @teclas.uy (from Instagram bio). Has a Medium blog (@maxtechera, 'Full Stack Engineer at @BetaLabs. #juniordevforlife'). Also has a second Instagram @max.techera (526 followers). Zoom meetings with Adam cover AAI time tracking, billing, QA reviews, and 1:1 syncs.

insights.summary:
Max Techera is the Director of Engineering at Last Rev / AnswerAI, operating as the technical backbone of the entire operation. Based in Montevideo, Uruguay (UTC-3), he's a fullstack engineer with 10+ years of experience, a BS in Computer Science, and a background in teaching (university professor — DIU, N6A courses, plus a recent AI notes workshop in Jan 2026) and writing (Medium blog). He manages engineering across multiple client projects (IAS, SmartNews, Diligent) while simultaneously leading AnswerAI product development — handling releases, npm publishing, Stripe billing, Langfuse tracing, Flagsmith feature flags, environment management, and team coordination.

His recent GitHub activity (Feb 2026) shows extremely active hands-on engineering on the-answerai/theanswer: merging PRs for MCP credential pattern fixes (AGENT-683), marketplace back button (AGENT-654), quicksetup canvas overwrites (AGENT-676), typecheck enablement (AGENT-679), metrics filter fixes (AGENT-678), version history auth fixes (AGENT-677), and IAS security critical CVE patches. He's the person who merges, deploys to staging, and promotes to production.

Max is also an AI tooling enthusiast — his recent repos include claude-plugins ('Simple, focused Claude Code plugins for developers and creators'), solo-claude, claude-max, and app-workshop-notas-ia (a workshop app for AI-powered notes, suggesting he's teaching AI workshops). He forked Microsoft's generative-ai-for-beginners course. He's a Zen Browser user (reported passkey issues). Co-creator of @teclas.uy (tech community/keyboard project from Uruguay). His earlier work spans React Native, GraphQL (Elixir, Ruby), Angular, .NET Core, Vue/Nuxt, and infrastructure (Terraform, RabbitMQ, Consul/Vault). He's a polyglot engineer who has touched nearly every layer of the stack.

Personally, he's a coffee enthusiast, tennis player (built an 'Elite Tennis Training Handbook' app deployed on Vercel), and someone who cares about his team. He's entrepreneurially minded and has cross-platform dotfiles (macOS and Arch Linux). Zoom meetings with Adam cover AAI time tracking, billing reviews, QA syncs, and strategic 3-way calls with Brad.
insights.bestApproach: Max communicates in rapid bursts — short messages, abbreviations, and direct asks. Match his energy: be concise and lead with the action needed. He's the engineering bottleneck for most projects, so respect his time — come with specifics (exact errors, PRs, branches) not vague descriptions. He responds well to peer-level technical discussion and appreciates when teammates are self-sufficient. He gets frustrated when scope creeps ('Im so done arguing, but we cannot have him work for 2 weeks on something and come out with 7000 changes where the actual goal is only 5% of that') and when communication is unclear. He's in Montevideo (UTC-3), typically available during US business hours with overlap. For urgent production issues, tag him directly — he'll respond and act fast.
motivators: AI tooling and developer productivity (Claude Code, agents)
stressors: scope creep and unfocused PRs ('7000 changes where the goal is only 5% of that')
arguing about code style vs substance
tools that add noise without value (SonarCloud)
managing multiple client accounts/credentials ('its hard to keep up with the amount of customers*services')
unclear communication or context-less requests
billing/invoicing overhead
being the single point of failure for releases
team members not following agreed-upon processes

personality.summary: Methodical, detail-oriented engineer who asks precise clarifying questions before diving into work. Direct communicator who surfaces blockers early and isn't afraid to question existing patterns. Self-sufficient problem solver who prefers to understand the full picture before acting.
personality.communicationStyle: Concise and technical. Leads with specific questions about architecture and data flow. Uses code snippets and links to illustrate points. Doesn't pad messages with pleasantries — gets straight to the point. Will tag specific people when he needs answers rather than broadcasting.
personality.bestApproach: Be direct and technical. Provide context about why something is the way it is, not just what to do. He appreciates understanding the reasoning behind decisions. Give him autonomy — he'll figure things out and come back with a plan. Async communication works well; he'll ping when ready.

Previously Tech Lead at Engie Factory, Senior Engineer at Toptal, and team lead at BitterSweet. 5.0/5.0 rating on Codementor with 50+ reviews. Personal email: camilo.rios.f@gmail.com. Has a daughter named Elena. GitHub account since 2013 with 26 public repos, all JavaScript. Known for building SaaS platforms, project management tools, and marketing sites. Works across multiple Last Rev client projects: Lively, Diligent, IAS, WoW, SmartNews.

insights.summary: Camilo is a reliable, results-oriented senior engineer who communicates with warm brevity. He's proactive about status updates (posts standup notes when missing meetings), collaborative in debugging sessions, and transparent about personal needs (sick days, doctor appointments, family). He works across multiple client projects simultaneously and manages context-switching well. His messages are short, polite, and action-oriented — he tags people directly, shares PR links immediately, and follows up on blockers without nagging. He's a team player who helps onboard others, shares environment configs, and volunteers to work weekends when behind.
communicationStyle: bilingual (English primary in Slack, Spanish native)
insights.bestApproach: Be direct and concise — Camilo communicates in short, action-focused messages. When requesting work, provide clear ticket links and Figma/design references. He appreciates when designs are locked before he starts coding. He's in Chile (UTC-3), so his best overlap with US East is morning/early afternoon EST. He's responsive to direct tags in Slack. For code reviews, just drop the PR link in #action-items and tag him.

designs changing after implementation starts

name: Krista Schlumpberger
title: Director of Support Services
notes: Based in Austin, TX. Previously at Marin Software / Perfect Audience. Played softball at Rogers State University. Active in Atlassian community (OpsGenie). Manages support team including ticket routing, Zendesk access, and client escalations. Coordinates with external client teams (e.g., Diligent, IAS). References Jira SUPPORT board tickets.

insights.summary:
Krista Schlumpberger is the Director of Support Services at Last Rev, functioning as a hybrid project manager, client success lead, and L1 support manager. She's the operational backbone of client engagements — managing Diligent, IAS, and WOW accounts simultaneously, coordinating deployments, routing support tickets, handling Contentful content entry, reviewing PRs, and serving as the primary point of contact for external stakeholders like Susan, Nicole, Derek, and Brad D.

Her Slack messages (14,900+) reveal someone who is deeply hands-on and relentlessly organized. She tracks ticket statuses across Jira (SUPPORT board), coordinates scheduling across timezones, proactively reassigns work based on bandwidth, and escalates technical issues to developers when needed. She's comfortable doing content work herself — uploading media, creating pages in Contentful, fixing content issues — while also managing her team.

Krista is direct and doesn't shy away from difficult conversations. She's openly frustrated when team members don't meet expectations ('I'm struggling to keep my cool with him right now... he is not engaged, shows up to stand up whenever he wants') and escalates management issues when needed. Despite this directness, she's warm and personable — using humor ('such a tough crowd', 'omg you almost got me'), celebration emojis (:partyparrot:, :tada:), and genuine thank-yous.

Previously at Marin Software / Perfect Audience in ad-tech. Played softball at Rogers State University. Based in Austin, TX (Central time).
insights.communicationStyle: Casual-professional with a warm, direct tone. Uses lowercase 'i', abbreviations (bc, TY, LMK, FYI), and ellipses between thoughts. Sends focused, action-oriented messages — often tagging specific people with specific asks. Comfortable escalating issues and being blunt about blockers. Uses exclamation marks and emojis naturally but sparingly. Writes in short bursts rather than long paragraphs.
insights.bestApproach: Be direct and action-oriented — Krista values clarity and follow-through above all. Lead with what you need and by when. If she asks for a status update, respond promptly with specifics (ticket number, PR link, timeline). She appreciates when people take ownership without being chased. Don't over-explain or add unnecessary context — she's juggling multiple client accounts and needs concise, actionable communication. Show appreciation for her coordination work; she's often the glue holding projects together. Best reached on Slack during CT morning/afternoon hours.
motivators: Resolving issues quickly — she jumps in personally when needed
Recognition for her coordination and operational work
Shipping quality work to clients like Diligent and IAS

Having to chase people for ticket updates
Scope creep from clients (e.g., 'even more added scope' from Derek)

Previously at Gartner (content marketing for C-suite) and CEB. Virginia Tech alum (BA Communication, PR concentration). Primary Last Rev contact for Diligent product page migration — drives page launches, coordinates design feedback from Abi Ravindra, manages nav updates, and tracks blockers. 463 Slack messages in workspace. Very hands-on with Sanity CMS content editing.

insights.summary:
Jordan Barlow is a seasoned brand marketing director who serves as the primary Diligent stakeholder driving the product page migration with Last Rev. She's a PMP-certified project manager at heart — organized, detail-oriented, and relentless about tracking blockers and action items. Her 463 Slack messages reveal someone who runs the project like a well-oiled machine: creating numbered blocker lists, using emoji status indicators (✅ done, ✏️ in progress, ❓ chasing), sending end-of-day recaps, and holding the team accountable to timelines. She bridges the gap between Diligent's design team (led by Abi Ravindra) and the Last Rev engineering team, translating design feedback into actionable requests.

She's hands-on in Sanity CMS — editing content, troubleshooting errors, testing pages, and flagging bugs (broken jump links, font inconsistencies, form picklist mismatches). She's not afraid to get into the weeds but also knows when to escalate ('She just told me to push it live today'). Her communication style is fast-paced, practical, and warm — she uses ':slightly_smiling_face:' frequently, softens direct asks with 'pls' and 'no rush', and self-deprecates when she makes mistakes ('OOPs', 'oh jk'). She values consistency ('consistency is my love language') and thoroughness.
communicationStyle: abbreviates-casually (rn, cuz, tbh, nvm, jk, diff, recco)
insights.bestApproach: Jordan is a project manager by nature — she responds best to structured, actionable communication. Lead with status updates and clear next steps. Use numbered lists when reporting multiple items. She tracks blockers meticulously, so proactively flag issues before she has to ask. She appreciates being kept in the loop ('pls keep this group informed') and values end-of-day summaries. She's in Eastern timezone and prefers to batch page launches strategically. When she asks 'what's the update here?' — respond promptly with specifics, not vague reassurances. She's warm and collaborative but has clear expectations about timelines.
motivators: consistency across brand touchpoints ('consistency is my love language')
empowering her team to self-serve in Sanity CMS
stressors: unclear timelines or vague status updates
design inconsistencies (fonts, spacing, colors not matching brand)
analytics gaps (not knowing if GA4 is tracking properly)
form/data issues (picklist mismatches, routing values not passing)
being the bottleneck for content updates across many pages
having to decode unclear feedback from internal stakeholders
technical errors in Sanity she can't resolve herself
conversationStarters: The product page migration — she's deeply invested in getting every page right
Her PMP certification — how project management training influences her marketing work
Brand consistency challenges across a large product portfolio
Her journey from Gartner content marketing to Diligent brand leadership
Training team members on Sanity CMS — she cares about enabling self-service
topicsToAvoid: Don't dismiss design feedback as minor — she takes brand consistency seriously
Avoid vague timelines — she wants specific dates and accountability
Don't assume she's non-technical — she's comfortable in Sanity CMS and debugging

name: Amanda Roesser
title: Digital Marketing Manager
notes: Amanda Jean Roesser is a B2B digital marketing professional at Diligent, managing the diligent.com website content, CTAs, product pages, and navigation updates. She coordinates closely with Last Rev on Sanity CMS content publishing and Netlify deployments. Previously Manager of Digital Marketing & Campaigns at Nintex where she achieved 6:1 to 8:1 pipeline-to-spend ratio and $2.2M Q2 pipeline. Also runs her own consultancy, Amanda Jean Growth Marketing. University of Denver graduate (Journalism, 2018) with Department Honours (3.75+ GPA). Won Nintex 'Builder of Bridges' and 'Marketer of the Month' awards (Dec 2024). Published author at 401k Specialist Magazine. Twitter: @AmandaJRoesser. WordPress blog: amandaroesser.wordpress.com.

education.honors: Department Honours, Dean's List, Hornbeck Scholar

insights.summary:
Amanda Roesser is a hands-on digital marketing manager at Diligent who owns the diligent.com web experience. She's the primary point of contact for website content changes — pushing product pages live, updating CTAs, managing navigation updates, coordinating Figma design handoffs, and implementing analytics tools like Crazy Egg. She works closely with Last Rev (Adam Harris, Krista) and her Diligent colleagues (Abi Ravindra, Derek Norton, Aled Bryon, Jordan Barlow) to keep the site current.

Her background is impressive: at Nintex she drove a 6:1–8:1 pipeline-to-spend ratio and $2.2M in quarterly pipeline, earning both 'Marketer of the Month' and 'Builder of Bridges' awards for cross-team collaboration. She graduated from University of Denver with honors in Journalism Studies and runs her own B2B growth marketing consultancy on the side.

In Slack, Amanda is action-oriented and direct. She sends requests with clear specificity — exact URLs, Figma links, copy details, and explicit asks. She's comfortable tagging people directly and follows up efficiently. She's collaborative but deadline-driven, often pushing to get things live quickly.
insights.bestApproach: Lead with action items and timelines — Amanda is results-driven and appreciates knowing exactly what's happening and when. Provide specific URLs and preview links when discussing web changes. She's used to coordinating between design (Figma), CMS (Sanity), and deployment (Netlify), so speak in those terms. Tag her directly for decisions. She's in Pacific time and responsive during business hours. When she asks for something to go live, she usually means ASAP.
motivators: getting web content live quickly and accurately
pipeline generation and measurable marketing ROI
stressors: delays in publishing content to production
unclear ownership of web changes
team being offline when urgent changes are needed
misalignment between design intent and implementation
broken or unpublished pages on diligent.com

personal: journalism and writing (DU Journalism graduate, WordPress blog)

conversationStarters: Her Nintex award-winning pipeline results — 6:1 to 8:1 ROI is exceptional
The Diligent AI page she's been building — interesting intersection of governance and AI
Her growth marketing consultancy — what kinds of B2B clients does she work with?
The homepage redesign project she's coordinating through Figma
University of Denver journalism background — does she still write?
topicsToAvoid: Don't second-guess her CTA or copy decisions without data — she's data-driven and has strong opinions backed by results
Avoid slow-rolling her publish requests — she escalates quickly when her team is offline

He's a client stakeholder who manages the Diligent marketing website and coordinates content migrations with the Last Rev team. His communication comes primarily through Jira/Zendesk support tickets rather than direct Slack messages, suggesting he operates through formal channels. He's considerate of others' workload ('If the scheduling increases the lift... please do that') but also holds teams accountable to deadlines ('you were due to give us a working go live date'). name: Vijay Murukesan
title: Web Analytics / QA Specialist
notes: Vijay Murukesan works at Diligent in a web analytics/QA capacity. Based in UK (Europe/London timezone). Active in the diligent-lr-external Slack channel. His messages focus on GA4 analytics testing, URL redirect validation, and QA sign-off for diligent.com pages. Coordinates with Jordan Barlow, Cameron Taylor (Last Rev), and Derek Norton (Diligent). Helped flag that Derek Norton had Slack connectivity issues and suggested email as fallback. Methodical approach — wants to verify tracking reports from GA4 after pages go live (24+ hours). No public LinkedIn, GitHub, or Twitter profile found under this exact name spelling. GitHub user 'vijay-brilliant-basics' found in search but unconfirmed match. insights.summary: Vijay Murukesan is a web analytics and QA professional at Diligent, based in the UK. He works on the marketing website (diligent.com), focusing on GA4 analytics verification, URL redirect testing, and page-level QA. His communication style is concise and task-oriented — he gives clear approvals ('approved'), flags issues with specific URLs, and coordinates across teams. He's methodical about testing, wanting 24+ hours of live data before validating tracking. He's helpful to teammates, proactively flagging communication issues (e.g., suggesting email when a colleague couldn't connect to Slack). Limited public web presence suggests he's a private, heads-down professional who focuses on execution rather than personal branding.
insights.bestApproach: Keep messages short and action-oriented — he communicates in brief, focused statements. When requesting QA sign-off, provide specific URLs and what to test. He's in UK timezone (GMT/BST), so morning US time overlaps with his afternoon. He appreciates when testing timelines are realistic (e.g., waiting for live data before validating). Tag him directly in Slack for attention.

communication tool issues (Slack connectivity)
testing URLs that redirect before they can be properly validated
time pressure on QA sign-off

Cross-team QA coordination between Last Rev and Diligent

insights.summary:
Aled Bryon is a Manager of Content Strategy at Diligent, based in Galway, Ireland. He's a seasoned content professional with 15+ years spanning journalism, content marketing, and PR. His background includes sports live blogging and managing a Twitter account with 119k followers — skills that translate into his current role where he produces polished, structured web content at pace.

In the Last Rev Slack workspace, Aled is highly active on the #diligent-lr-external channel where he drives the Diligent website content buildout. He builds pages directly in Sanity CMS, drafts copy in Google Docs, and coordinates closely with Krista (support), Adam Harris (engineering lead), and Vijay (Diligent internal). He's hands-on — populating role pages, segment pages, industry pages, and use case pages himself rather than delegating. He introduced himself as being "'cross the pond in Galway" showing self-awareness of the timezone difference.

His communication reveals someone who is organized, persistent, and deadline-driven. He follows up on outstanding items regularly, flags bugs proactively (misaligned CTAs, random 'test' text appearing), and provides clear status updates. He thinks in terms of page templates and content architecture — suggesting component improvements and testing CTA variations (e.g., 'Take a guided tour' vs 'Request a demo'). He's comfortable pushing back diplomatically when needed and escalating blockers.
insights.bestApproach: Aled is organized and expects the same in return. Always respond with clear timelines and status updates — he'll follow up if you don't. Provide links to specific Sanity pages or Google Docs when discussing content. He's in Europe/Dublin timezone (GMT/IST), so schedule meetings before noon his time if possible. He appreciates when technical issues are acknowledged quickly and fixed promptly. Don't make him chase — proactive updates earn trust. He's self-sufficient in Sanity CMS so just needs publishing/deployment support from the engineering side.
motivators: clear page templates and component architecture
stressors: unresolved bugs blocking page launches
lack of status updates from engineering
Slack navigation (prefers MS Teams)
corporate IT restrictions (Google Drive blocked by Diligent IT)
unclear ownership of page components
delayed publishing timelines
conversationStarters: The Diligent website content buildout — he's driving a massive amount of page creation
His journalism background — sports live blogging with 119k Twitter followers is impressive
Content strategy at scale — how he approaches role pages, segment pages, and use case templates
Life in Galway — he's clearly proud of being 'cross the pond'
CTA testing philosophy — he has opinions on 'Request a demo' vs action-oriented alternatives
topicsToAvoid: Don't complain about Slack — he already finds it frustrating compared to MS Teams
Avoid vague timelines — he needs specific dates and will hold you to them
Don't assume he needs hand-holding in Sanity — he builds pages himself

Background in content/social channel management, digital governance, and employee social media training. Brooklyn-based. 2K LinkedIn followers, 500+ connections. Volunteers with Catchafire (pro bono marketing consulting for civil rights orgs) and New Kings Democrats (Communications and Data Committee). Active LinkedIn engager — likes posts about B2B marketing, paid ads trends, AI in marketing, and ecommerce. Primary contact for Diligent marketing website projects with Last Rev — manages homepage A/B tests, migration, analytics, localization, and form/Marketo integrations.

insights.summary:
Adam Cohen is the Senior Director of Growth at Diligent, serving as the primary client stakeholder for the Diligent marketing website managed by Last Rev. He describes himself as an 'operator and deep generalist obsessed with the internet and ecommerce' and holds a Product Management Certification from Mind the Product. Based in Brooklyn, NY, he's deeply involved in the day-to-day of the Diligent website — from homepage A/B testing (Optimizely), analytics debugging (GA4), migration planning, localization/UKI expansion, Marketo form integrations, and Sanity CMS permissions management.

His Slack messages reveal a detail-oriented, analytically-minded marketer who catches issues proactively — he spotted broken session duration metrics, identified cross-domain data contamination (Brainloop pages leaking into Diligent data), and flagged UX issues like navigation behavior. He's comfortable diving into technical details (brand values, form field mappings, A/B test split ratios) while maintaining a marketing/business perspective. He coordinates across his internal Diligent team (Derek Norton, Sarah Freedman, Vijay Murukesan, John Habib) and the Last Rev team.

Outside work, he volunteers with Catchafire doing pro bono marketing consulting for civil rights organizations and serves on the Communications and Data Committee for New Kings Democrats, a Brooklyn political organization. His LinkedIn activity shows interest in B2B marketing trends, paid ads strategy, AI in marketing, and ecommerce.
insights.bestApproach: Adam is a sharp, detail-oriented client who notices everything — broken metrics, misaligned UI elements, cross-domain data leaks. Come prepared with specifics and data when presenting work. He appreciates quick turnarounds and proactive communication. When he reports an issue, he usually includes context (screenshots, URLs, timestamps), so match that level of detail in your responses. He prefers Slack but also uses email (and has asked for email follow-ups for colleagues without Slack access). Respect his marketing expertise — he thinks in terms of conversion tracking, A/B test validity, and campaign attribution. Eastern timezone.
stressors: broken analytics or metrics (session duration spikes, missing brand values)
cross-domain data contamination
slow response times on urgent fixes
team members without proper tool access (Slack, Sanity)
unclear ownership of technical issues
scheduling conflicts with internal meetings

The homepage A/B test results — did the new variant win?
His Mind the Product certification — how has it shaped his approach to growth?
Pro bono consulting work with Catchafire — what kind of orgs does he help?
topicsToAvoid: Don't downplay analytics discrepancies — he takes data accuracy very seriously
Avoid vague timelines on deliverables — he tracks everything closely

location: United States (West Coast, likely SF Bay Area/California)
notes: Co-Founder of Strong365 Lab, a youth mental health organization. Has MPH degree (University of Washington). 20+ years cross-sector experience in marketing, corporate strategy, and product development before transitioning to public health. Personal connection to mental health — brother diagnosed with schizophrenia. Co-authored chapter in 'Intervening Early in Psychosis' (American Psychiatric Publishing, 2019). FINRA securities licenses (7, 24, 63, 22) from prior finance career. Extensive volunteer work: Active Minds grad mentor (2016-present), Felton Institute board member, Flawless Foundation advisory board, yoga instructor for Hamilton Families (homeless transition). Has a daughter (~12 years old as of 2026). Guest in Last Rev Slack as single-channel guest in #strong-365-external. Invited by Cameron (ULH6GM1LP). 639 Slack messages. Works with Mili Mehta (co-founder) and coordinates with Krista at Last Rev. Site hosted on Squarespace, domain via GoDaddy. Partners include Northwell Health.

insights.summary:
Chantel Garrett is the Co-Founder of Strong365 Lab, a youth mental health nonprofit focused on reaching young people before crisis through digital campaigns and behavioral science. She has an MPH from the University of Washington and 20+ years of cross-sector experience spanning Fortune 100 marketing/corporate strategy, finance (FINRA licensed), and public health. Her mission is deeply personal — her brother was diagnosed with schizophrenia as a young Marine, which catalyzed her career pivot to mental health advocacy.

In Slack, Chantel is a polished, professional client who communicates with warmth and efficiency. She's courteous and appreciative (frequent 'thank you' and 'tysm'), uses emojis naturally but sparingly (:pray:, :slightly_smiling_face:, :raised_hands:, :rolling_on_the_floor_laughing:), and is direct about needs and timelines without being demanding. She proactively manages meetings — canceling when there's no agenda, rescheduling with specific time options, and following up on outstanding items. She references her co-founder Mili frequently, suggesting a collaborative decision-making style.

Her background in marketing and corporate strategy shows in how she communicates — she's organized, deadline-aware, and understands project management language (tickets, launches, tracking). She's not technical but is comfortable with technical concepts (CNAME records, archive files, hosting services). She's a mother with a daughter and balances professional intensity with personal warmth.

stressors: partner/funder deadlines (Northwell)
unclear project status
funding uncertainty
technical issues blocking launches

conversationStarters: The Sway campaign — how is the next phase of funding going?
Her published chapter on early psychosis intervention
Strong365's digital campaign results — what's resonating with youth?
Her yoga background — does she still practice/teach?
The Agents of Change Summit where she was featured as a changeagent
insights.bestApproach: Be professional but warm — Chantel appreciates courtesy and clear communication. Lead with status updates and next steps. She manages her calendar proactively and prefers to cancel meetings when there's no agenda rather than waste time. Always follow through on invoices and deliverables promptly — she tracks outstanding items. Include Mili in significant decisions as they operate as a team. She's in Pacific time and prefers morning/afternoon slots.
topicsToAvoid: Don't be overly casual too quickly — she maintains professional warmth
Avoid technical jargon without explanation — she's not a developer
Don't delay on invoicing or deliverables — she tracks these closely

Bo Mohazzabi is a serial entrepreneur and seasoned sales leader who is now Co-Founder/CEO of HaystacksAI (haystax.dev), a B2B AI startup. He has deep roots in the SaaS sales world with 14+ years of experience, having held enterprise sales and leadership roles at Optimizely, Amplitude Analytics, and KarmaCheck before founding his current company. Brad added Bo to the #gtm channel in April 2025, and meeting notes show Bo participating in daily GTM strategy meetings focused on pricing, client outreach (reply campaigns), and Heroku deployments. He coordinates with Brad on pricing calls, content review, and messaging alignment for the blog automation project.

His career shows a pattern of founding and scaling B2B sales organizations — Stampt (acquired by Mobivity), CityMint, New Era Sales Consulting, and now HaystacksAI. He's described as someone who 'prides himself in exceeding goals' and for whom 'fluff and sales jargon make me cringe' — suggesting a direct, no-nonsense communicator. For GTM discussions, speak in his language: pipeline, pricing, outreach sequences, demo calls.

socials.note: Medium confidence — name matches but unverified

insights.summary:
Diana Slaney is a PMP and CSM certified Technical Program Manager who previously worked as a contractor at Last Rev, primarily on the IAS (Integral Ad Science) account. She specialized in Jira workflow optimization, data visualization dashboards, billing/invoicing reports, and process documentation. She is detail-oriented and organized — creating onboarding checklists, scoping questionnaires, FRDs, training guides, and launch checklists.

Diana lived in Italy for a period (her WhatsApp is an Italian +39 number and she peppers messages with Italian phrases like "perfetto!" and "Ciao!"). She left Last Rev with a warm farewell in January 2024 but stayed connected. In 2025, she reached out to Adam for help with job searching — first asking about Toptal, then requesting a reference for Clever Real Estate, where she landed a Program Manager role in June 2025.

Her communication style is warm, enthusiastic, and grateful. She uses lots of exclamation marks, emojis, and expressive language ("awwww!!!", "soooo much"). She is professional in her work messages — tagging specific people, sharing Google Docs, scheduling meetings, and following up proactively — but wraps it all in genuine warmth and appreciation.

stressors: unclear data and messy Jira configurations
tax season
job searching uncertainty

conversationStarters: Congratulate her on the Clever Real Estate role — she was thrilled to get it
Ask about life in Italy — she lived there and clearly loved it
Her approach to Jira workflow optimization and data visualization
How she is settling into the new Program Manager role at Clever Real Estate
insights.bestApproach: Diana is warm and appreciative — match her energy with friendly, supportive communication. She is highly organized and will follow up proactively. When working together, she appreciates clear action items and deadlines but delivers them with warmth. She values personal relationships alongside professional ones — ask about her life, not just work. She is deeply grateful when people help her and will reciprocate.
topicsToAvoid: Do not push on why she left Last Rev — she departed gracefully and on good terms
Avoid tax/financial topics unless she brings them up

name: Mili Mehta

name: Aakash Relan
title: Director, Business Transformation
notes: Key IAS stakeholder for Last Rev relationship. Business transformation leader with PMI, ITIL, Six Sigma background. Previously at Tripadvisor, Deel, PMI.

insights.summary: Aakash is a highly structured program manager who runs cross-functional workstreams with precision. Across 332 Slack messages, his dominant pattern is clear: he opens with greetings (27%), makes polite requests ('could you please' is his signature phrase), and closes with appreciation (46% of messages include 'thank you'). He's the connective tissue between IAS leadership and the Last Rev/AnswerAI engineering team — constantly tagging the right people, summarizing meeting outcomes, and ensuring alignment. His longer messages are structured with bullet points, action items, and clear ownership assignments. He rarely uses emoji (under 1%) and almost never uses assertive/demanding language — instead defaulting to diplomatic phrasing like 'could you please' and 'wanted to follow up.' When things stall, he follows up persistently but politely. His most impressive quality: after every call, he sends a structured recap with action items, owners, and deadlines — a program management instinct that makes him easy to work with if you match his rigor.

stressors: tools resetting/losing state
delayed responses
unclear ownership
misalignment between teams

communicationFingerprint.signaturePhrase: Thank you! / Could you please / Let us know / Make sure
communicationFingerprint.structurePattern: Long messages use bullet points with owner tags and deadlines
communicationFingerprint.recapBehavior: Posts structured meeting summaries after calls with action items and owners

activityPattern.activeDays: Tuesday and Wednesday highest, fairly even Mon-Fri
activityPattern.lateActivity: Occasional messages 5-8 PM CT, very rare after 8 PM
activityPattern.channels: 85% in #answeragent-ias-lr-external, 6% in #ias-ask-aai-support, rest in group DMs

technicalDepth.productionVsStaging: 18 production / 15 staging — actively involved in deployment decisions
technicalDepth.note: Comfortable discussing APIs, staging/production environments, and security configs. Not a developer but deeply technical for a program manager.

conversationStarters: Ask about the Fiddler guardrails rollout — it's been a multi-month initiative he's driven
Reference the Langfuse analytics deep-dive he requested — shows you're tracking his priorities
Ask about the security vulnerability remediation — he personally tracked every item
Mention his structured meeting recaps — he'd appreciate knowing they're valued
HBR Advisory Council — ask what insights he's finding interesting lately
insights.bestApproach: Mirror his structure: use bullet points, assign clear owners, include deadlines. Lead with status updates. He responds best to organized, specific communication — not vague updates. Always close the loop on action items; he tracks everything. When he asks 'could you please confirm,' he means it — respond explicitly. Tag him directly rather than expecting him to catch ambient updates. Morning CT is his peak responsiveness window.
topicsToAvoid: Past tool failures/resets without resolution context
Vague timelines without concrete dates
Leaving his questions unanswered — he will follow up
warningSignals: Multiple follow-ups on the same item = urgency escalating
When he CCs leadership or uses group DMs with managers = topic has become exec-visible
When he opens with 'Just wanted to follow up' for the 2nd+ time = patience wearing thin
When he starts summarizing 'where we are' unprompted = he feels things are off-track
